meta.title: The Obvious Problem That No One Can Agree On
meta.author: Veritasium

questions.question: In Newcomb's paradox, when does the supercomputer put $1 million in the mystery box?
options: After you make your choice
If it predicted you would take only the mystery box
If it predicted you would take both boxes
Only when you promise to one-box
questions.answer: If it predicted you would take only the mystery box

questions.question: At approximately what predictor accuracy (C) do one-boxing and two-boxing have equal expected utility?
Which decision theory uses probabilities based on prior evidence of the predictor's accuracy and favors one-boxing?
options: Causal decision theory
Strategic dominance theory
Evidential decision theory
Expected value theory

questions.question: Why do two-boxers argue that your choice cannot change the contents of the mystery box?
options: The predictor can read your mind and will refill the box
The prediction and box contents were fixed before you entered the room
Taking both boxes always yields more
The supercomputer updates its prediction in real time
questions.answer: The prediction and box contents were fixed before you entered the room

questions.question: What did Gibbard and Harper argue in their 1978 paper?
options: The rational act is to one-box and one-boxers fare better
The rational act is to two-box, though two-boxers fare worse, and the game is rigged
Evidential and causal decision theory always agree
Newcomb's paradox has no bearing on rationality
questions.answer: The rational act is to two-box, though two-boxers fare worse, and the game is rigged

questions.question: According to the material, when would a committed two-boxer choose to one-box?
options: Never; two-boxers always take both boxes
Only when the predictor is less than 50% accurate
If there are multiple trials, if they can pre-commit, or if their choice could causally change the past
Only in the game of chicken
questions.answer: If there are multiple trials, if they can pre-commit, or if their choice could causally change the past

questions.question: Who originated Newcomb's paradox?
options: Robert Nozick
Gibbard and Harper
William Newcomb
Robert McNamara

questions.question: In the prisoner's dilemma analogy, what happens when the game is played repeatedly rather than once?
options: Defection always pays more
Cooperation can be better; rational society may be full of cooperators
The dominant strategy switches to one-boxing
Expected utility no longer applies
questions.answer: Cooperation can be better; rational society may be full of cooperators

questions.question: What is strategic dominance in the context of Newcomb's paradox?
options: The strategy that has higher expected utility when the predictor is accurate
A strategy that is better in every possible state of the world; two-boxing is dominant in the causal view
The one-boxer's use of prior evidence
The predictor's ability to anticipate your choice
questions.answer: A strategy that is better in every possible state of the world; two-boxing is dominant in the causal view

questions.question: Robert McNamara, as US Secretary of Defense, advocated what strategy that later became known as MAD?
options: Disarmament and diplomacy only
Assured destruction: deter attack by maintaining the ability to inflict unacceptable damage
First-strike capability to eliminate the USSR
The doomsday device from Dr. Strangelove
questions.answer: Assured destruction: deter attack by maintaining the ability to inflict unacceptable damage

questions.question: When did the Soviet Union conduct its first nuclear weapons test (RDS-1)?
options: August 29, 1949
Mid-1960s

questions.question: In the game of chicken, what is described as the best strategy?
options: Always swerve to avoid collision
Wait for the other driver to commit first
Visibly remove the steering wheel so the opponent sees you cannot swerve
Randomly choose to swerve or not
questions.answer: Visibly remove the steering wheel so the opponent sees you cannot swerve

questions.question: What does the "Why Ain'cha Rich?" argument highlight?
options: That one-boxers are irrational
The tension between the rational act and the best outcome
That the predictor is always accurate
The importance of strategic dominance
questions.answer: The tension between the rational act and the best outcome

questions.question: According to the material, what is the core issue at the heart of Newcomb's paradox?
options: Whether the predictor can read minds
Whether a strong correlation that you know is not causal should influence your decision
Whether free will exists
Whether expected utility is the right way to decide
questions.answer: Whether a strong correlation that you know is not causal should influence your decision

questions.question: In Dr. Strangelove (1963), what is the purpose of the tampering kill switch on the doomsday device?
options: To prevent enemies from disabling the device
To prevent the Russians themselves from having second thoughts
To allow the US to trigger it remotely
To delay the explosion by 24 hours
questions.answer: To prevent the Russians themselves from having second thoughts

questions.question: Robert Nozick is cited in the material for what observation about Newcomb's paradox?
options: That one-boxing is clearly rational
That two-boxing is the dominant strategy
That people find the correct choice obvious but split almost evenly and think the other side is silly
That the paradox disproves free will
questions.answer: That people find the correct choice obvious but split almost evenly and think the other side is silly

questions.question: What common thread links Newcomb's paradox and mutually assured destruction (MAD)?
options: Both involve nuclear weapons
Both were invented in the 1970s
The best outcome follows from pre-committing to a seemingly worse option
Both rely on evidential decision theory
questions.answer: The best outcome follows from pre-committing to a seemingly worse option
